channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.eurogamer.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.eurogamer.net

item.title: No, Mixtape will not be delisted "due to music licenses expiring"
item.content:encoded:
Mixtape publisher Annapurna Interactive has laid out in no uncertain terms that no, Mixtape will not be delisted "due to music licenses expiring".

Talking to Kotaku, creative director Johnny Galvatron confirmed that, unlike other games like Guitar Hero that use licensed music and later had to remove them or be delisted entirely, Beethoven and Dinosaur paid "extra" to keep "Mixtape’s licenses up in perpetuity".

Then, just to make sure there was no misunderstanding, the publisher stressed on X/Twitter: "We heard some people say Mixtape would be delisted due to music licenses expiring. That was a lie. Have a great weekend, everyone."

We heard some people say MIXTAPE would be delisted due to music licenses expiring.

That was a lie.

Have a great weekend, everyone. https://t.co/C3VesfWPpz

— Annapurna Interactive (@A_i) May 16, 2026

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

Elsewhere in the interview, Galvatron talked about the risk of "hat[ing all the songs] by the end" of the game's production cycle, but that turned out to be a unnecessary fear.

"My biggest hesitation was like, ‘if I make a game with all of my favourite songs, is it gonna be like starting a business with my friends? Am I gonna hate everything by the end?’ And no, I don’t," Galvatron added.

"I still get a buzz listening to it and starting the game and seeing Devo play and these characters come out."

We thought Mixtape was "a delight", writing in Eurogamer's four out of five stars Mixtape review: "It's a celebration of teenage life that makes its point, aptly, just as a teenager would".

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/mixtape-music-license-delisted
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Phasmophobia admits it "missed the mark" and prepares "to earn back this community’s trust"
item.content:encoded:
"Your feedback and reviews were justified."

Phasmophobia maker Kinetic Games has apologised to players for issues with its long-awaited 'Player Character Update', admitting it "missed the mark" and "didn't deliver on our promises to you".

In numerous patches and hotfixes released to address the many issues spawned by the latest update that released earlier this week, the studio explained how it had been working through player feedback to provide "a much better experience for everyone playing Phasmophobia".

"We want to start today by apologising for our Player Character Update that launched last week. It’s clear we missed the mark on this, and didn’t deliver on our promises to you," the team said.

"Your feedback and your reviews were justified. Our team has grown significantly over the last year, and with that comes a higher standard that, in this case, we failed to meet."

Now, in a bid to address that feedback, Kinetic has already deployed one substantive patch and two hotfixes to bring the game "in-line with our vision for this update".

"We've read feedback from across the entire community. The frustration and disappointment has been clear," the team admitted.

If you are one of the of people who love both the nightmare-inducing ghosts of Phasmophobia and the surreal world of troubled author Alan Wake, don't forget that a three-week collaboration between the two horror series is now live.

It takes place in Phasmophobia - it's called Phasmophobia by Alan Wake - and it will see locations from Alan's second outing (Alan Wake 2) recreated in Phasmophobia alongside new character skins, community challenges "and more".

Mentioned in this article

Phasmophobia

Meta Quest, Nintendo Switch 2, PC, PS5, PlayStation VR2, Xbox Series X/S



https://www.eurogamer.net/phasmophobia-character-update-apology
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Terraria marks 70 million sales and 15th anniversary with a promise to "continue beyond" its next big update
item.content:encoded:
"You all are the engine that makes this all work."

Terraria developer Re-Logic has insisted that it is committed to ensuring "the world of Terraria remains and will remain vibrant" as the sandbox game marks its 15th anniversary.

Sitting in 70 million game libraries across PC, console, and mobile – a milestone developer Re-Logic calls "equal parts insane and humbling" – the team vowed that "Terraria updates will continue beyond 1.4.6/Crossplay".

The team has also been working on "a few items" to mark the anniversary, including a (mysterious) 15th anniversary collector's edition and Terraria Design Works, a retrospective look at the life of Terraria, developed in collaboration with Lost in Cult.

"It is really hard to put into words what your support for these many years has meant to our little studio," the studio wrote in an update on Steam.

"Simply put, you all are the engine that makes this all work. Your support allows us to keep going, keep expanding upon Terraria, without having to fall back to price increases or microtransactions. This is becoming increasingly rare in modern gaming, and we cannot thank you enough for making what we do every day possible.

"From the hardcore players with thousands of hours to those just discovering our 'metroidvania in a sandbox' today... from the builders to the speedrunners... from those that we interact with regularly in our community to those that just enjoy the game in silence... from those that are regular Terraria enjoyers to those that like to shake things up with all sorts of mods on tModloader... each and every one of you are precious to us."

The statement concluded: "We hope that our game has brought you some joy over the years. Terraria is a unique experience one that we like to think that we have shaped together alongside the fans into the awesomeness that it is today. This is as much your journey as it is ours!"

The Design Works book is set to go on sale on 28th May. The collector's edition has yet to be dated, but Re-Logic promises you can "expect to receive full details and for pre-orders to open up sometime in June".

Mentioned in this article

Terraria

Android, Mac, Nintendo 3DS, Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Wii U, PC, PS3, PS4, PlayStation Vita, Stadia, Windows Phone, Xbox 360, Xbox One, iOS



https://www.eurogamer.net/terraria-70m-sales-15-anniversary
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox is now XBOX, apparently
item.content:encoded:
Xbox has been rebranded as, er, XBOX.

No, that's not me shouting at you, but is, in fact, how the company may be branding Xbox from here on in. Let me explain.

New CEO Asha Sharma popped up on X/Twitter during the week to poll players on their preference between Xbox and its shoutier sibling, XBOX. 19,000 votes later, XBOX won with almost 65 percent of the votes.

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

Two days after that, Sharma shared a screenshot of the refreshed official Xbox page, which shows the caplocked title: XBOX. Interestingly, though, the change hasn't yet made it to the company's official website or indeed any other social media channels on Facebook, Insta, BlueSky, and so on.

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

It is, of course, just the latest in what's becoming a long line of tweaks and changes. Sharma recently revealed the company would be sunsetting the company's Copilot initiative, which was once envisioned as an AI-driven "personal gaming sidekick", designed to offer real-time advice in games such as Minecraft.

Sharma has also recently admitted Xbox "needs to move faster", and "deepen [its] connection with the community, and address friction for both players and developers".

According to another report – and hints from Sharma – Microsoft is rolling out a new tier of Game Pass specifically aimed at Chinese gamers.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/xbox-is-now-xbox
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Subnautica 2's Early Access roadmap is here
item.content:encoded:
Subnautica 2 developer Unknown Worlds has outlined what to expect in the early to mid-term of its early access release.

With over two million players jumping in at the deep end, Unknown World has unveiled its "early access roadmap", detailing what the team has to work through on its To Do List.

While Unknown Worlds is unwilling to provide solid dates at this point, the team does reveal that first up will be planned improvements to Biomods system, blight encounters, wrecks gameplay, vehicle docking and fabrication, PDA databank, and voicelogs priority system. You can also expect more passive Biomod slots, changes to the storage cache, and sprinting.

The second update will focus on co-op, including HUD signals, base builder tool, and a pinned recipes system, as well as additions to voice chat, emote, player trading, player revive, and unspecified "additional customisations".

While all this is going on, you can also look out for "ongoing improvements" to bug fixes, balance tuning, optimisation, and other continuous adjustments, as well as – a little later on – "major expansion updates", including a new region, new biomes, new creatures, resources, tools, a new vehicle, and the "next chapter of the story".

"So, as we build towards the next big drop that expands on the world, biomes, creatures, resources, tools, vehicles, and brings you the next chapter of the story, we'll be planning on adding several improvement updates in the meantime!" the studio wrote. "The entire dev team has been working hard to put together some great quality-of-life fixes for our first update. This should help adjust and address some areas that need a little tweaking to make your Subnautica 2 experience even better.

"And for those of you who have been choosing co-op to adventure together with your friends, we've got some requested improvements and additions coming in our second update."

When you first crash-land in Subnautica 2, you might feel a little lost. Don't worry though – we're here to walk you through exactly what things you should do first in Subnautica 2 and, hopefully, help you avoid getting eaten...

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/subnautica-2s-early-access-roadmap-is-here
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
